behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on real filesystem watch semantics across directory boundaries (subdirectory events under a watched root) and on proving an externally visible side effect (the file is actually processed). Unit-only or pure in-process fakes can miss watcher/backend differences. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence appears in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against real input/output directories, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert via output/readback (or observable processing artifact) that it is processed without manual triggering.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily cross-module state/timing handoff (request intake + dedup store/window + pipeline enqueue), which can fail even if unit tests for the dedup function pass. Real OS/network/subprocess is not explicitly required by the behavior text, and no AI/ML output is involved. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because the behavior text does not require real external contract checks or real-infra-only effects; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by absence of model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire daemon intake + dedup + queue modules in-process with a controllable clock, submit two requests for the same path within the configured window, and assert only one pipeline execution occurs; then advance beyond the window and verify a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly cites OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events, which is real infrastructure behavior that mocks often fail to reproduce faithfully. It also requires round-trip proof of externally visible side effect count (exactly once processing). Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML or multi-agent decision chain is present.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon with a real watcher on a real filesystem, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple OS events, and assert from observable outputs/logged processing IDs that only one processing run occurred for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation while a live watcher runs is OS/filesystem behavior across process lifetime; correctness depends on real watcher re-subscription behavior and externally observable resumed processing. In-process mocks can hide this failure mode. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because the behavior text contains no AI/ML-dependent decision or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, remove the watched directory, recreate it at the same path while daemon stays running, add a new file, and verify successful processing without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: The behavior couples concurrent arrival with filesystem creation timestamp ordering; real filesystem timestamp granularity/event timing can differ from fakes. To avoid false confidence, the ordering should be validated via real write/readback processing order. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model output quality or multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: With the daemon running on real directories, create multiple files in a tight window, capture their filesystem creation timestamps and observed processing completion/order, and assert processing order matches timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real file-write lifecycle and watcher timing/race behavior at the OS boundary; mocks often hide partial-write timing issues. The requirement is an externally visible side effect condition (no processing before write completion, processing after completion) best proven by round-trip observation. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML-dependent behavior is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real filesystem, begin a slow/streamed write to a file that triggers arrival early, verify no processing artifact appears during the write, then finish/close the file and verify processing occurs afterward.
